Lab3

Pedro Avila, Javier Benitez, Brandon Rivera

2026-03-13

library(corrplot)
library(caret)
library(lmtest)
library(dplyr)
library(stringi)

1. Descargue el conjunto de datos.

load("Listings.RData")

head(listings, 5)
##      id                        listing_url    scrape_id last_scraped
## 1  5456  https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/5456 2.025092e+13   2025-09-17
## 2  6448  https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/6448 2.025092e+13   2025-09-17
## 3  8502  https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/8502 2.025092e+13   2025-09-17
## 4 13035 https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/13035 2.025092e+13   2025-09-17
## 5 22828 https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/22828 2.025092e+13   2025-09-16
##        source                                               name
## 1 city scrape          Walk to 6th, Rainey St and Convention Ctr
## 2 city scrape Secluded Studio @ Zilker - King Bed, Bright & Airy
## 3 city scrape                            Woodland Studio Lodging
## 4 city scrape      Historic house in highly walkable East Austin
## 5 city scrape                 Garage Apartment central SE Austin
##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   description
## 1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Great central  location for walking to Convention Center, Rainey Street, East 6th Street, Downtown, Congress Ave Bats.<br /><br />  Free wifi<br /><br />No Smoking,  No pets
## 2                                                                                                                           Clean, private space with everything you need for a quiet, comfy, private stay close to Zilker Park and Barton Springs, the river, parks, trails, and downtown. King bed, vaulted ceilings, high-speed fiber internet. Quality furnishings and amenities will make you feel at home. We offer contactless check-in/checkout, if you like (and we are vaccinated).
## 3 Studio rental on lower level of home located in a 1950s neighborhood less than two miles from downtown Austin and close to bus routes.<br /><br />On stays less than 30 nights additional Austin city hotel taxes of 11% will be collected separately following confirmation of reservation.<br /><br />Texas state hotel taxes will be collected by Airbnb.<br /><br />Hotel taxes apply for all stays of 29 nights or less.  No hotel taxes are charged for rentals of 30 nights or more.
## 4                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         Comfortable 2 bedroom/2 bathroom home very centrally located on Austin’s East Side. Walk to downtown, Whole Foods, Target, CVS, plus endless local establishments and attractions here on the East Side.<br /><br />Sorry, no pets.
## 5                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             Fully furnished, centrally located, second story garage apartment (studio) for monthly rentals.
##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         neighborhood_overview
## 1 My neighborhood is ideally located if you want to walk to bars and restaurants downtown, East 6th Street or Rainey Street.  The Convention Center is only 3 1/2 blocks away and a quick 10 minute walk. Whole foods store located 5 blks , easily walkable.
## 2                                                                                                                                                        The neighborhood is fun and funky (but quiet)! People are friendly  and you can't beat the location.
## 3                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
## 4    East Cesar Chavez is a gentrifying urban area that remains highly diverse. Guests can walk to downtown, the Convention Center, Lady Bird Lake, Rainey Street, E. 6th Street, Franklin Barbecue, taco trailers, coffee shops, hot spots, and hidden gems.
## 5                                                                                                                                                                                                             wikipedia: East_Riverside-Oltorf,_Austin,_Texas
##                                                                                               picture_url
## 1                                         https://a0.muscache.com/pictures/14084884/b5a35a84_original.jpg
## 2 https://a0.muscache.com/pictures/airflow/Hosting-6448/original/a0ab6e9c-58ed-4d57-acb1-60cb68b068e0.jpg
## 3   https://a0.muscache.com/pictures/miso/Hosting-8502/original/be48ea3b-6d8a-4d9b-bc13-8aa514ef246a.jpeg
## 4  https://a0.muscache.com/pictures/miso/Hosting-13035/original/00407f5f-ff1f-4653-8832-4fbeee901a83.jpeg
## 5  https://a0.muscache.com/pictures/miso/Hosting-22828/original/91072e92-a641-4c30-85ee-4b527c6ccac8.jpeg
##   host_id                                host_url    host_name host_since
## 1    8028  https://www.airbnb.com/users/show/8028       Sylvia 2009-02-16
## 2   14156 https://www.airbnb.com/users/show/14156          Amy 2009-04-20
## 3   25298 https://www.airbnb.com/users/show/25298        Karen 2009-07-11
## 4   50793 https://www.airbnb.com/users/show/50793 Margaret Ann 2009-11-02
## 5   56488 https://www.airbnb.com/users/show/56488        David 2009-11-22
##   host_location
## 1    Austin, TX
## 2    Austin, TX
## 3    Austin, TX
## 4    Austin, TX
## 5    Austin, TX
##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  host_about
## 1 I am a licensed Real Estate Broker and owner of Armadillo Realty.  I attended The University of Texas at Austin and fell in love with the small town that it was back in 1979; I have been here every since.  I love the Art, Music and Film scene here in Austin.  There is so much natural beauty to enjoy as well. I especially enjoy Barton Springs Pool in the summertime along with the Zilker Hillside theater productions. SXSW, Austin City Limits Festival and the East Austin Art Studio Tour are among my favorite events. I also enjoy a sunset cruise on my canoe to Congress bridge to see the Mexican Freetail Bats come out for their nightly feeding.  
## 2                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      We are a family of four (with teenagers, all of us vaccinated). We love our home town and location... can't beat the park, river, and downtown. We love having guests in our garage apartment... it's fun to talk to people about their lives, hometowns, and travels. We're happy to give recommendations and want you to be comfy.
## 3                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  I handle the reservations at the studio on the lower level of a house that belongs to a good friend of mine.  I really enjoy this part of town, and it is great to be offering comfortable & homey lodging for people coming from around the world to experience Austin.
## 4                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       We're a responsible, easygoing couple who enjoy Austin's friendly atmosphere, good food, and great music. We enjoy gardening, reading, travel, and renovating our hundred-year-old home bit by bit.\n\nOur best times in Austin are on bikes--you can see a lot, get some sun, and get around downtown without the hassle of a car.
## 5                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       Wyoming native living in Austin since 1996. I like to garden, read, cook, and work on DIY projects.

2. Haga un análisis exploratorio extenso de los datos.

Explique bien todos los hallazgos. No ponga solo gráficas y código. Debe llegar a conclusiones interesantes para poder predecir. Explique el preprocesamiento que necesitó hacer

2.1. Descripción de las variables

summary(listings)

cual_nominales <- c(
    "listing_url",
    "source",
    "name",
    "description",
    "neighborhood_overview",
    "picture_url",
    "host_url",
    "host_name",
    "host_location",
    "host_about",
    "host_is_superhost",
    "host_thumbnail_url",
    "host_picture_url",
    "host_neighbourhood",
    "host_verifications",
    "host_has_profile_pic",
    "host_identity_verified",
    "neighbourhood",
    "neighbourhood_cleansed",
    "neighbourhood_group_cleansed",
    "bathrooms_text",
    "amenities",
    "has_availability",
    "license",
    "instant_bookable",
    "city"
)

cual_ordinales <- c(
    "last_scraped",
    "host_response_time",
    "host_since",
    "property_type",
    "room_type",
    "calendar_last_scraped",
    "first_review",
    "last_review"
)


cuant_discretas <- c(
    "host_listings_count",
    "host_total_listings_count",
    "accommodates",
    "bedrooms",
    "beds",
    "minimum_nights",
    "minimum_nights_avg_ntm",
    "maximum_nights",
    "maximum_nights_avg_ntm",
    "minimum_minimum_nights",
    "maximum_minimum_nights",
    "availability_30",
    "availability_60",
    "availability_90",
    "availability_365",
    "number_of_reviews",
    "number_of_reviews_ltm",
    "number_of_reviews_l30d",
    "availability_eoy",
    "number_of_reviews_ly",
    "estimated_occupancy_l365d",
    "reviews_per_month",
    "minimum_maximum_nights",
    "maximum_maximum_nights"
)

cuant_continuas <- c(
    "host_response_rate",
    "host_acceptance_rate",
    "latitude",
    "longitude",
    "bathrooms",
    "price",
    "review_scores_rating",
    "review_scores_accuracy",
    "review_scores_cleanliness",
    "review_scores_checkin",
    "review_scores_communication",
    "review_scores_location",
    "review_scores_value",
    "calculated_host_listings_count",
    "calculated_host_listings_count_entire_homes",
    "calculated_host_listings_count_private_rooms",
    "calculated_host_listings_count_shared_rooms",
    "estimated_revenue_l365d"
)

cuantitativas <- c(cuant_discretas, cuant_continuas)
cuantitativas
##  [1] "host_listings_count"                         
##  [2] "host_total_listings_count"                   
##  [3] "accommodates"                                
##  [4] "bedrooms"                                    
##  [5] "beds"                                        
##  [6] "minimum_nights"

##  [8] "maximum_nights"                              
##  [9] "maximum_nights_avg_ntm"                      
## [10] "minimum_minimum_nights"                      
## [11] "maximum_minimum_nights"

## [14] "availability_90"

datos_cuant <- listings[, cuantitativas]
head(datos_cuant, 5)

cualitativas <- c(cual_nominales, cual_ordinales)
cualitativas
##  [1] "listing_url"                  "source"                      
##  [3] "name"                         "description"                 
##  [5] "neighborhood_overview"        "picture_url"                 
##  [7] "host_url"                     "host_name"                   
##  [9] "host_location"                "host_about"                  
## [11] "host_is_superhost"            "host_thumbnail_url"          
## [13] "host_picture_url"             "host_neighbourhood"          
## [15] "host_verifications"           "host_has_profile_pic"        
## [17] "host_identity_verified"       "neighbourhood"               
## [19] "neighbourhood_cleansed"       "neighbourhood_group_cleansed"
## [21] "bathrooms_text"               "amenities"                   
## [23] "has_availability"             "license"                     
## [25] "instant_bookable"             "city"                        
## [27] "last_scraped"                 "host_response_time"          
## [29] "host_since"                   "property_type"               
## [31] "room_type"                    "calendar_last_scraped"       
## [33] "first_review"                 "last_review"
datos_cual <- listings[, cualitativas]
head(datos_cual, 5)
##                          listing_url      source
## 1  https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/5456 city scrape
## 2  https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/6448 city scrape
## 3  https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/8502 city scrape
## 4 https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/13035 city scrape
## 5 https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/22828 city scrape
##                                                 name
## 1          Walk to 6th, Rainey St and Convention Ctr
## 2 Secluded Studio @ Zilker - King Bed, Bright & Airy
## 3                            Woodland Studio Lodging
## 4      Historic house in highly walkable East Austin
## 5                 Garage Apartment central SE Austin
##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   description
## 1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Great central  location for walking to Convention Center, Rainey Street, East 6th Street, Downtown, Congress Ave Bats.<br /><br />  Free wifi<br /><br />No Smoking,  No pets
## 2                                                                                                                           Clean, private space with everything you need for a quiet, comfy, private stay close to Zilker Park and Barton Springs, the river, parks, trails, and downtown. King bed, vaulted ceilings, high-speed fiber internet. Quality furnishings and amenities will make you feel at home. We offer contactless check-in/checkout, if you like (and we are vaccinated).
## 3 Studio rental on lower level of home located in a 1950s neighborhood less than two miles from downtown Austin and close to bus routes.<br /><br />On stays less than 30 nights additional Austin city hotel taxes of 11% will be collected separately following confirmation of reservation.<br /><br />Texas state hotel taxes will be collected by Airbnb.<br /><br />Hotel taxes apply for all stays of 29 nights or less.  No hotel taxes are charged for rentals of 30 nights or more.
## 4                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         Comfortable 2 bedroom/2 bathroom home very centrally located on Austin’s East Side. Walk to downtown, Whole Foods, Target, CVS, plus endless local establishments and attractions here on the East Side.<br /><br />Sorry, no pets.
## 5                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             Fully furnished, centrally located, second story garage apartment (studio) for monthly rentals.
##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         neighborhood_overview
## 1 My neighborhood is ideally located if you want to walk to bars and restaurants downtown, East 6th Street or Rainey Street.  The Convention Center is only 3 1/2 blocks away and a quick 10 minute walk. Whole foods store located 5 blks , easily walkable.
## 2                                                                                                                                                        The neighborhood is fun and funky (but quiet)! People are friendly  and you can't beat the location.
## 3                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
## 4    East Cesar Chavez is a gentrifying urban area that remains highly diverse. Guests can walk to downtown, the Convention Center, Lady Bird Lake, Rainey Street, E. 6th Street, Franklin Barbecue, taco trailers, coffee shops, hot spots, and hidden gems.
## 5                                                                                                                                                                                                             wikipedia: East_Riverside-Oltorf,_Austin,_Texas
##                                                                                               picture_url
## 1                                         https://a0.muscache.com/pictures/14084884/b5a35a84_original.jpg
## 2 https://a0.muscache.com/pictures/airflow/Hosting-6448/original/a0ab6e9c-58ed-4d57-acb1-60cb68b068e0.jpg
## 3   https://a0.muscache.com/pictures/miso/Hosting-8502/original/be48ea3b-6d8a-4d9b-bc13-8aa514ef246a.jpeg
## 4  https://a0.muscache.com/pictures/miso/Hosting-13035/original/00407f5f-ff1f-4653-8832-4fbeee901a83.jpeg
## 5  https://a0.muscache.com/pictures/miso/Hosting-22828/original/91072e92-a641-4c30-85ee-4b527c6ccac8.jpeg
##                                  host_url    host_name host_location
## 1  https://www.airbnb.com/users/show/8028       Sylvia    Austin, TX
## 2 https://www.airbnb.com/users/show/14156          Amy    Austin, TX
## 3 https://www.airbnb.com/users/show/25298        Karen    Austin, TX
## 4 https://www.airbnb.com/users/show/50793 Margaret Ann    Austin, TX
## 5 https://www.airbnb.com/users/show/56488        David    Austin, TX
##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  host_about
## 1 I am a licensed Real Estate Broker and owner of Armadillo Realty.  I attended The University of Texas at Austin and fell in love with the small town that it was back in 1979; I have been here every since.  I love the Art, Music and Film scene here in Austin.  There is so much natural beauty to enjoy as well. I especially enjoy Barton Springs Pool in the summertime along with the Zilker Hillside theater productions. SXSW, Austin City Limits Festival and the East Austin Art Studio Tour are among my favorite events. I also enjoy a sunset cruise on my canoe to Congress bridge to see the Mexican Freetail Bats come out for their nightly feeding.  
## 2                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      We are a family of four (with teenagers, all of us vaccinated). We love our home town and location... can't beat the park, river, and downtown. We love having guests in our garage apartment... it's fun to talk to people about their lives, hometowns, and travels. We're happy to give recommendations and want you to be comfy.
## 3                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  I handle the reservations at the studio on the lower level of a house that belongs to a good friend of mine.  I really enjoy this part of town, and it is great to be offering comfortable & homey lodging for people coming from around the world to experience Austin.
## 4                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       We're a responsible, easygoing couple who enjoy Austin's friendly atmosphere, good food, and great music. We enjoy gardening, reading, travel, and renovating our hundred-year-old home bit by bit.\n\nOur best times in Austin are on bikes--you can see a lot, get some sun, and get around downtown without the hassle of a car.
## 5                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       Wyoming native living in Austin since 1996. I like to garden, read, cook, and work on DIY projects.

## 1                 t
## 2                 t
## 3                 f
## 4                 t
## 5                 t

## 2 ["Private patio or balcony", "Stove", "Cleaning products", "First aid kit", "Wine glasses", "Iron", "Free washer \\u2013 In building", "Ceiling fan", "Private entrance", "Outdoor furniture", "Hot water", "Shower gel", "Outlet covers", "Conditioner", "Refrigerator", "Dishes and silverware", "Self check-in", "Dining table", "Free parking on premises", "Keypad", "Wifi", "Hot water kettle", "Outdoor dining area", "Hair dryer", "Room-darkening shades", "Bed linens", "Cooking basics", "TV", "Long term stays allowed", "Mini fridge", "Baking sheet", "Central air conditioning", "Oven", "Free dryer \\u2013 In building", "Children\\u2019s books and toys", "Pack \\u2019n play/Travel crib", "Smoke alarm", "Babysitter recommendations", "Central heating", "Essentials", "Clothing storage: closet and dresser", "Ethernet connection", "Luggage dropoff allowed", "Changing table", "Dedicated workspace", "Fire extinguisher", "Crib", "Shampoo", "Body soap", "Extra pillows and blankets", "Coffee maker", "Toaster", "Carbon monoxide alarm", "Laundromat nearby", "Record player", "Microwave", "Kitchen", "Board games", "Hangers", "Private backyard \\u2013 Fully fenced", "Free street parking"]
## 3                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        ["Wifi", "Smoke alarm", "Shampoo", "Heating", "Hair dryer", "Pets allowed", "Essentials", "Host greets you", "Free street parking", "Hot water", "Kitchen", "Central air conditioning"]
## 4                                                                                                                                                                ["Private patio or balcony", "Coffee", "Stove", "Cleaning products", "First aid kit", "Bathtub", "Wine glasses", "Iron", "Blender", "Ceiling fan", "Private entrance", "Outdoor furniture", "Dishwasher", "Hot water", "Shower gel", "Dishes and silverware", "Conditioner", "Refrigerator", "Freezer", "Coffee maker: drip coffee maker, pour-over coffee", "Self check-in", "Dining table", "Wifi", "Hot water kettle", "Heating", "Hair dryer", "Bed linens", "Cooking basics", "TV", "Long term stays allowed", "Baking sheet", "Central air conditioning", "Oven", "Dryer", "Smoke alarm", "Shared backyard \\u2013 Fully fenced", "Free washer \\u2013 In unit", "Essentials", "Clothing storage: closet and dresser", "Free driveway parking on premises \\u2013 2 spaces", "Exterior security cameras on property", "Dedicated workspace", "Fire extinguisher", "Lockbox", "Shampoo", "Body soap", "Sound system with aux", "Extra pillows and blankets", "Toaster", "Free street parking", "Carbon monoxide alarm", "Microwave", "Kitchen", "Hangers"]
## 5                                                                                                                                                                                                                       ["AC - split type ductless system", "Cleaning products", "First aid kit", "Iron", "Free washer \\u2013 In building", "Ceiling fan", "Private entrance", "Hot water", "Dishes and silverware", "Refrigerator", "Freezer", "Dining table", "Portable fans", "Wifi", "Drying rack for clothing", "Hot water kettle", "Hair dryer", "Room-darkening shades", "Bed linens", "Cooking basics", "Long term stays allowed", "Baking sheet", "Oven", "Free dryer \\u2013 In building", "Smoke alarm", "Essentials", "43 inch HDTV with Amazon Prime Video, Apple TV, Disney+, HBO Max, Hulu, Netflix", "Heating - split type ductless system", "Host greets you", "Other electric stove", "Luggage dropoff allowed", "Books and reading material", "Exterior security cameras on property", "Clothing storage: walk-in closet, closet, and dresser", "Fire extinguisher", "Extra pillows and blankets", "Coffee maker", "Toaster", "Free street parking", "Laundromat nearby", "Microwave", "Kitchen", "Hangers"]
##   has_availability license instant_bookable          city last_scraped
## 1                t    <NA>                f Austin, Texas   2025-09-17
## 2                t    <NA>                t Austin, Texas   2025-09-17
## 3                t    <NA>                f Austin, Texas   2025-09-17
## 4                t    <NA>                f Austin, Texas   2025-09-17
## 5                t    <NA>                f Austin, Texas   2025-09-16
##   host_response_time host_since      property_type       room_type
## 1 within a few hours 2009-02-16  Entire guesthouse Entire home/apt
## 2     within an hour 2009-04-20  Entire guesthouse Entire home/apt
## 3       within a day 2009-07-11 Entire guest suite Entire home/apt
## 4     within an hour 2009-11-02        Entire home Entire home/apt
## 5     within an hour 2009-11-22  Entire guesthouse Entire home/apt
##   calendar_last_scraped first_review last_review
## 1            2025-09-17   2009-03-19  2025-09-02
## 2            2025-09-17   2011-09-06  2025-08-20
## 3            2025-09-17   2010-02-19  2025-05-05
## 4            2025-09-17   2011-03-16  2025-07-31
## 5            2025-09-16   2010-03-16  2025-08-16

Variables cuantitativas discretas no decidimos clasificarlas id , scrape_id , host_id. Adicionalmente la variable calendar_updated se decidio no incluirla ya que toda su data es NA

2.2. Análisis exploratorio

library(ggplot2)

dim(listings)
## [1] 171748     80
str(listings)
## 'data.frame':    171748 obs. of  80 variables:
##  $ id                                          : num  5456 6448 8502 13035 22828 ...
##  $ listing_url                                 : chr  "https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/5456" "https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/6448" "https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/8502" "https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/13035" ...
##  $ scrape_id                                   : num  2.03e+13 2.03e+13 2.03e+13 2.03e+13 2.03e+13 ...
##  $ last_scraped                                : chr  "2025-09-17" "2025-09-17" "2025-09-17" "2025-09-17" ...
##  $ source                                      : chr  "city scrape" "city scrape" "city scrape" "city scrape" ...
##  $ name                                        : chr  "Walk to 6th, Rainey St and Convention Ctr" "Secluded Studio @ Zilker - King Bed, Bright & Airy" "Woodland Studio Lodging" "Historic house in highly walkable East Austin" ...
##  $ description                                 : chr  "Great central  location for walking to Convention Center, Rainey Street, East 6th Street, Downtown, Congress Av"| __truncated__ "Clean, private space with everything you need for a quiet, comfy, private stay close to Zilker Park and Barton "| __truncated__ "Studio rental on lower level of home located in a 1950s neighborhood less than two miles from downtown Austin a"| __truncated__ "Comfortable 2 bedroom/2 bathroom home very centrally located on Austin’s East Side. Walk to downtown, Whole Foo"| __truncated__ ...
##  $ neighborhood_overview                       : chr  "My neighborhood is ideally located if you want to walk to bars and restaurants downtown, East 6th Street or Rai"| __truncated__ "The neighborhood is fun and funky (but quiet)! People are friendly  and you can't beat the location." "" "East Cesar Chavez is a gentrifying urban area that remains highly diverse. Guests can walk to downtown, the Con"| __truncated__ ...
##  $ picture_url                                 : chr  "https://a0.muscache.com/pictures/14084884/b5a35a84_original.jpg" "https://a0.muscache.com/pictures/airflow/Hosting-6448/original/a0ab6e9c-58ed-4d57-acb1-60cb68b068e0.jpg" "https://a0.muscache.com/pictures/miso/Hosting-8502/original/be48ea3b-6d8a-4d9b-bc13-8aa514ef246a.jpeg" "https://a0.muscache.com/pictures/miso/Hosting-13035/original/00407f5f-ff1f-4653-8832-4fbeee901a83.jpeg" ...
##  $ host_id                                     : int  8028 14156 25298 50793 56488 215917 230747 272156 268988 378744 ...
##  $ host_url                                    : chr  "https://www.airbnb.com/users/show/8028" "https://www.airbnb.com/users/show/14156" "https://www.airbnb.com/users/show/25298" "https://www.airbnb.com/users/show/50793" ...
##  $ host_name                                   : chr  "Sylvia" "Amy" "Karen" "Margaret Ann" ...
##  $ host_since                                  : chr  "2009-02-16" "2009-04-20" "2009-07-11" "2009-11-02" ...
##  $ host_location                               : chr  "Austin, TX" "Austin, TX" "Austin, TX" "Austin, TX" ...
##  $ host_about                                  : chr  "I am a licensed Real Estate Broker and owner of Armadillo Realty.  I attended The University of Texas at Austin"| __truncated__ "We are a family of four (with teenagers, all of us vaccinated). We love our home town and location... can't bea"| __truncated__ "I handle the reservations at the studio on the lower level of a house that belongs to a good friend of mine.  I"| __truncated__ "We're a responsible, easygoing couple who enjoy Austin's friendly atmosphere, good food, and great music. We en"| __truncated__ ...

summary(listings)

datos_cuant <- listings[, cuantitativas]
datos_cual  <- listings[, cualitativas]


listings$price <- as.numeric(gsub("[$,]", "", listings$price))
listings$host_response_rate <- as.numeric(gsub("%", "", listings$host_response_rate))
listings$host_acceptance_rate <- as.numeric(gsub("%", "", listings$host_acceptance_rate))

summary(datos_cuant)

##  Max.   :60.00000                            Max.   :12750000       
##                                              NA's   :95502
ggplot(listings, aes(x = price)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40)

ggplot(listings, aes(x = review_scores_rating)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30)

ggplot(listings, aes(x = reviews_per_month)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30)

ggplot(listings, aes(y = price)) +
  geom_boxplot()

ggplot(listings, aes(y = reviews_per_month)) +
  geom_boxplot()

Podemos ver que los datos los cuales principalmente podriamos hacer analisis numerico llegan a tener sesgos cada uno por su lado

shapiro.test(sample(na.omit(listings$price), 5000))
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  sample(na.omit(listings$price), 5000)
## W = 0.11981, p-value < 2.2e-16
shapiro.test(sample(na.omit(listings$review_scores_rating), 5000))
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  sample(na.omit(listings$review_scores_rating), 5000)
## W = 0.52732, p-value < 2.2e-16
shapiro.test(sample(na.omit(listings$reviews_per_month), 5000))
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  sample(na.omit(listings$reviews_per_month), 5000)
## W = 0.74653, p-value < 2.2e-16

Haciendo la prueba de shapiro-wilks podemos darnos cuenta por que el p-value llega a estar mas abajo que el alpha autoimpuesto de 0.5, podemos darnos cuenta que no es normal.

2.3. Análisis de relaciones con la variable respuesta

library(corrplot)

vars_interes <- c("price", "accommodates", "bedrooms", "beds", "bathrooms",
                  "review_scores_rating", "reviews_per_month",
                  "availability_365", "estimated_occupancy_l365d",
                  "estimated_revenue_l365d", "number_of_reviews")

cor_data <- listings[, vars_interes]
cor_data <- cor_data[complete.cases(cor_data), ]

cor_matrix <- cor(cor_data)

corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper",
         addCoef.col = "black", tl.col = "black", tl.srt = 45,
         mar = c(0,0,1,0))

Las variables accommodates, bedrooms, beds y bathrooms tienen la mayor correlación positiva con price. Las variables de reseñas y ocupación muestran correlaciones débiles.

par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

plot(listings$accommodates, listings$price,
     main = "Price vs Accommodates",
     xlab = "Capacidad", ylab = "Precio")
abline(lm(price ~ accommodates, data = listings), col = "black", lwd = 2)

plot(listings$bedrooms, listings$price,
     main = "Price vs Bedrooms",
     xlab = "Habitaciones", ylab = "Precio")
abline(lm(price ~ bedrooms, data = listings), col = "black", lwd = 2)

plot(listings$bathrooms, listings$price,
     main = "Price vs Bathrooms",
     xlab = "Baños", ylab = "Precio")
abline(lm(price ~ bathrooms, data = listings), col = "black", lwd = 2)

plot(listings$review_scores_rating, listings$price,
     main = "Price vs Review Score",
     xlab = "Calificación", ylab = "Precio")
abline(lm(price ~ review_scores_rating, data = listings), col = "black", lwd = 2)

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

A mayor tamaño de la propiedad, mayor precio. La calificación no muestra relación clara con el precio.

boxplot(price ~ room_type, data = listings,
        main = "Precio por tipo de habitación",
        xlab = "Tipo", ylab = "Precio",
        ylim = c(0, 500))

kruskal.test(price ~ room_type, data = listings)
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  price by room_type
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 8225.6, df = 3, p-value < 2.2e-16

Los alojamientos completos tienen precios más altos. Kruskal-Wallis confirma que las diferencias son significativas (p < 0.05).

boxplot(price ~ city, data = listings,
        main = "Precio por ciudad",
        xlab = "Ciudad", ylab = "Precio",
        ylim = c(0, 500),
        las = 2)

kruskal.test(price ~ city, data = listings)
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  price by city
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 8184.1, df = 6, p-value < 2.2e-16

El precio varía según la ciudad, siendo la ubicación un factor relevante (p < 0.05).

Conclusión: Los mejores predictores del precio son accommodates, bedrooms, bathrooms, room_type y city.

3. Incluya un análisis de grupos en el análisis exploratorio. (Javi)

Explique las características de cada uno.

4. Divida el set de datos preprocesados (Brandon)

Dividalos en dos conjuntos: Entrenamiento y prueba. Describa el criterio que usó para crear los conjuntos: número de filas de cada uno, estratificado o no, balanceado o no, etc.

set.seed(123)

pricing_without_na <- listings[!is.na(listings$price), ]

trainIndex <- createDataPartition(pricing_without_na$price, p = .7, list = FALSE)
train <- pricing_without_na[trainIndex, ]
test <- pricing_without_na[-trainIndex, ]
# Tratamiento de outliers en 'price' (IQR) sin perder filas:
# En vez de eliminar registros (lo que puede dejar 0 casos para entrenar),
# recortamos (winsorizamos) el precio a los límites IQR calculados en TRAIN.

Q1 <- quantile(train$price, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
Q3 <- quantile(train$price, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
IQR_value <- Q3 - Q1

low  <- as.numeric(Q1 - 1.5 * IQR_value)
high <- as.numeric(Q3 + 1.5 * IQR_value)

cat("IQR bounds price (train):", low, "to", high, "\n")
## IQR bounds price (train): -189 to 635
cat("Rows before -> train:", nrow(train), ", test:", nrow(test), "\n")
## Rows before -> train: 53373 , test: 22873
# Clip/Winsorize
train$price <- pmin(pmax(train$price, low), high)
test$price  <- pmin(pmax(test$price,  low), high)

cat("Rows after  -> train:", nrow(train), ", test:", nrow(test), "\n")
## Rows after  -> train: 53373 , test: 22873
cat("NA counts -> price/train:", sum(is.na(train$price)), ", bedrooms/train:", sum(is.na(train$bedrooms)), "\n")
## NA counts -> price/train: 0 , bedrooms/train: 81

Entrenamos con el 70% de los datos y probamos con el 30% restante. La partición se hizo de manera estratificada para mantener la distribución del precio en ambos conjuntos. Luego, se aplicó un recorte (winsorización) de outliers en price con límites IQR calculados en entrenamiento, para reducir el impacto de valores extremos sin eliminar observaciones.

5. Haga ingeniería de características (Brandon)

¿qué variables cree que puedan ser mejores predictores para el precio de las casas? Explique en que basó la selección o no de las variables.

Prueba de Supuestos

Nuestros supuestos son los siguientes: 1. Los cuatros (bedrooms) son linealmente relacionados con el precio. 2. Los baños (bathrooms) son linealmente relacionados con el precio. 3. La calificación de las reseñas (review_scores_rating) es linealmente relacionada con el precio.

train_bedrooms <- train[complete.cases(train[, c("price", "bedrooms")]), ]
train_bathrooms <- train[complete.cases(train[, c("price", "bathrooms")]), ]
train_reviews <- train[complete.cases(train[, c("price", "review_scores_rating")]), ]

model_bedrooms <- lm(price ~ bedrooms, data = train_bedrooms)
model_bathrooms <- lm(price ~ bathrooms, data = train_bathrooms)
model_ReviewScore <- lm(price ~ review_scores_rating, data = train_reviews)

Linealidad

Podemos observar que los residuos no muestran un patrón claro, lo que sugiere que la relación entre las variables independientes y el precio es aproximadamente lineal. Sin embargo, hay cierta dispersión en los residuos, lo que podría indicar que la relación no es perfectamente lineal.

par(mfrow = c(1, 3))
plot(
  fitted(model_bedrooms),
  resid(model_bedrooms),
  main = "Linealidad - Bedrooms"
)
abline(h = 0, col = "red")

plot(
  fitted(model_bathrooms),
  resid(model_bathrooms),
  main = "Linealidad - Bathrooms"
)
abline(h = 0, col = "red")

plot(
  fitted(model_ReviewScore),
  resid(model_ReviewScore),
  main = "Linealidad - Review Score"
)
abline(h = 0, col = "red")

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

Normalidad de los residuos

Segun la prueba de Shapiro-Wilk, los residuos de los modelos no siguen una distribución normal, lo que podría afectar la validez de las inferencias basadas en estos modelos. Sin embargo, dado el tamaño de la muestra, es posible que esta falta de normalidad no tenga un impacto significativo en la capacidad predictiva del modelo. Muy seguramente, la falta de normalidad se deba a la presencia de valores atípicos o a una distribución sesgada de los residuos, lo que es común en datos de precios.

Regla de decisión: * Si p-value ≤ 0.05 → Se rechaza H₀ (evidencia de no normalidad).

sample_resid_bedrooms <- sample(resid(model_bedrooms), 5000)
sample_resid_bathrooms <- sample(resid(model_bathrooms), 5000)
sample_resid_ReviewScore <- sample(resid(model_ReviewScore), 5000)

shapiro.test(sample_resid_bedrooms)
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  sample_resid_bedrooms
## W = 0.90705, p-value < 2.2e-16
shapiro.test(sample_resid_bathrooms)
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  sample_resid_bathrooms
## W = 0.91878, p-value < 2.2e-16
shapiro.test(sample_resid_ReviewScore)
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  sample_resid_ReviewScore
## W = 0.85344, p-value < 2.2e-16

Hipótesis:

  • H₀: Los residuos no siguen una distribución normal.
  • H₁: Los residuos siguen una distribución normal.

Homocedasticidad

Observamos que en nuestro caso de Review Score, los residuos muestran una mayor dispersión a medida que aumentan los valores ajustados, lo que sugiere la presencia de heterocedasticidad. En los casos de Bedrooms y Bathrooms, la dispersión de los residuos parece ser más constante, aunque también muestra cierta variabilidad.

Regla de decisión: * Si p-value > 0.05 → No se rechaza H₀ (varianza constante). * Si p-value ≤ 0.05 → Se rechaza H₀ (existe heterocedasticidad).

bptest(model_bedrooms)
## 
##  studentized Breusch-Pagan test
## 
## data:  model_bedrooms
## BP = 5527.4, df = 1, p-value < 2.2e-16
bptest(model_bathrooms)
## 
##  studentized Breusch-Pagan test
## 
## data:  model_bathrooms
## BP = 3308.4, df = 1, p-value < 2.2e-16
bptest(model_ReviewScore)
## 
##  studentized Breusch-Pagan test
## 
## data:  model_ReviewScore
## BP = 39.836, df = 1, p-value = 2.762e-10

Hipótesis:

  • H₀: La varianza de los errores es constante (homocedasticidad).
  • H₁: La varianza de los errores no es constante (heterocedasticidad).

Independencia

En el caso de todos los modelos, el valor de Durbin-Watson tiene un p value menor a 0.05, lo que indica que hay evidencia de autocorrelación de primer orden en los residuos. Esto sugiere que los errores no son independientes, lo que podría afectar la validez de las inferencias basadas en estos modelos. * Si p-value ≤ 0.05 → Se rechaza H₀ (evidencia de autocorrelación).

dwtest(model_bedrooms)
## 
##  Durbin-Watson test
## 
## data:  model_bedrooms
## DW = 1.4031, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true autocorrelation is greater than 0
dwtest(model_bathrooms)
## 
##  Durbin-Watson test
## 
## data:  model_bathrooms
## DW = 1.4533, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true autocorrelation is greater than 0
dwtest(model_ReviewScore)
## 
##  Durbin-Watson test
## 
## data:  model_ReviewScore
## DW = 1.6713, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true autocorrelation is greater than 0

Hipótesis:

  • H₀: No existe autocorrelación de primer orden en los residuos.
  • H₁: Existe autocorrelación de primer orden en los residuos.

6. Todos los resultados deben ser reproducibles por lo que debe fijar que los conjuntos de entrenamiento y

prueba sean los mismos siempre que se ejecute el código.

7. Modelo univariado de regresión lineal (Javi)

Seleccione una de las variables y haga un modelo univariado de regresión lineal para predecir el precio de las casas. Analice el modelo (resumen, residuos, resultados de la predicción). Muéstrelo gráficamente.

8. Modelo de regresión lineal (Brandon)

Haga un modelo de regresión lineal con todas las variables numéricas para predecir el precio de las casas. Analice el modelo (resumen, residuos, resultados de la predicción). Muestre el modelo gráficamente.

model_full <- lm(price ~ ., data = train[, cuantitativas])
summary(model_full)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = price ~ ., data = train[, cuantitativas])
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -1885.95   -69.69   -18.96    46.39   715.43

## bedrooms                                      1.182e+01  8.584e-01  13.773

## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 113.4 on 41209 degrees of freedom
##   (12122 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.5334, Adjusted R-squared:  0.5329 
## F-statistic:  1149 on 41 and 41209 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
pred_full <- predict(model_full, newdata = test[, cuantitativas])
actual_full <- test$price

9. Analice el modelo. (Pedro)

Determine si hay multicolinealidad entre las variables, y cuáles son las que aportan al modelo. Haga un análisis de correlación de las características del modelo y especifique si el modelo se adapta bien a los datos. Explique si hay sobreajuste (overfitting) o no. En caso de existir sobreajuste, haga otro modelo que lo corrija.

10. Si tiene multicolinealidad o sobreajuste, haga un modelo con las variables que sean mejores predictoras

del precio de las casas. Determine la calidad del modelo realizando un análisis de los residuos. Muéstrelo gráficamente.

11. Elabore un modelo en el que utilice al menos una de las técnicas de regularización (Javi)

(Ridge, Lasso, ElasticNet) compare los resultados con los otros modelos.

12. Utilice cada modelo con el conjunto de prueba (Brandon)

Determine la eficiencia del algoritmo para predecir el precio de las casas. ¿Qué tan bien lo hizo? ¿Qué medidas usó para determinar la calidad de la predicción?

r2_test <- function(actual, predicted) {
  ss_res <- sum((actual - predicted)^2, na.rm = TRUE)
  ss_tot <- sum((actual - mean(actual, na.rm = TRUE))^2, na.rm = TRUE)
  1 - ss_res / ss_tot
}

# Remove NA values for fair comparison
valid_idx <- complete.cases(pred_full, actual_full)
pred_full_valid <- pred_full[valid_idx]
actual_full_valid <- actual_full[valid_idx]

results_full <- data.frame(
  Modelo = c("Regresión Lineal Completa"),
  RMSE = c(
    caret::RMSE(pred_full_valid, actual_full_valid)
  ),
  R2 = c(
    r2_test(actual_full_valid, pred_full_valid)
  )
)

results_full
##                      Modelo     RMSE        R2
## 1 Regresión Lineal Completa 112.0029 0.5298918

13. Discuta sobre la efectividad de los modelos. (Pedro)

¿Cuál lo hizo mejor? ¿Cuál es el mejor modelo para predecir el precio de las casas? Haga los gráficos que crea que le pueden ayudar en la discusión.
